condition_precedents: Should-cost model built for top 5 raw material categories
Alternative supplier/specification qualified by R&D
Procurement and supply-chain risk assessment signed off
industry_sources: FICCI — FMCG India Report 2024 (ficci.in/sector/61/) — raw material cost as % of FMCG revenue and procurement benchmarks
McKinsey & Company — Unlocking Value in FMCG Supply Chains (mckinsey.com/industries/consumer-packaged-goods) — COGS reduction levers 5-15%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct BOM review for top-20 SKUs by revenue: map every material input to specification, supplier, price, and YoY cost trend
Build should-cost models for primary material categories using commodity indices and benchmark specs; identify formulation trade-offs
Qualify 1–2 alternative suppliers per key material; run competitive RFQ with incumbent vs alternative to establish negotiation tension
Lock 12–18 month pricing agreements with indexed reopeners; implement material cost variance reporting in monthly S&OP

diagnostic_signals.signal: Key material cost as % of net sales >45% vs category benchmark indicating formulation or procurement gap
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Direct materials savings pool is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Single-source supply dependency for >30% of material value creating pricing leverage for supplier
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Dual-sourcing will restore competitive tension and reduce cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Material price variance unfavourable >5% for ≥3 consecutive quarters vs plan
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Procurement strategy or hedging requires reset

required_data_fields: Bill of materials for top-20 revenue SKUs with quantity per unit and unit prices
Material cost trend data (24 months) by input category
Supplier concentration data (number of qualified sources per key material)
Commodity price indices for key materials
Formulation specification constraints from R&D

condition_precedents: SKU profitability analysis completed (net margin per SKU)
Commercial / marketing approval for tail SKU discontinuation
Production planning updated to reflect simplified range
industry_sources: FICCI — FMCG India Industry Survey 2024 (ficci.in) — SKU complexity and tail rationalization data
McKinsey & Company — SKU Rationalization in CPG (mckinsey.com/industries/consumer-packaged-goods) — 10-20% SKU reduction improving margins 2-4%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build SKU-level P&L for all active SKUs: net revenue, variable COGS, and contribution margin; flag SKUs with margin <5% or volume <5 units/day
Validate rationalization candidates against consumer need (cover unique consumption occasion) and retailer requirements before delisting
Develop delisting plan with retailer communication, inventory liquidation, and production run-out schedule
Track manufacturing complexity reduction (changeover time, run length), working capital improvement, and margin uplift post-rationalisation

diagnostic_signals.signal: SKU count in category >2× nearest competitor with ≥20% of SKUs contributing <2% of category revenue
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Long-tail SKUs are adding complexity cost without proportionate revenue contribution

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average production run length <50% of minimum economic run due to SKU proliferation-driven changeovers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: SKU rationalisation will improve manufacturing efficiency

diagnostic_signals.signal: New SKU launch success rate <40% (measured 12 months post-launch vs plan), indicating proliferation is not creating value
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Focused portfolio strategy will improve ROII

required_data_fields: SKU-level net revenue, volume, and contribution margin (12 months)
Manufacturing setup/changeover data by SKU and line
Retailer ranging and space allocation by SKU
Consumer research on SKU redundancy within portfolio
Inventory level and days-on-hand by SKU

condition_precedents: Consumer research on pack-size price sensitivity completed
Revenue management team capability in place
Trade terms aligned with new architecture
industry_sources: Nielsen — India FMCG Market Pulse 2024 (nielsen.com/insights/) — price-pack architecture and value tier benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build price-value waterfall by SKU and channel: map RSP → distributor margin → trade promotion → net realisation → contribution
Identify price-pack architecture gaps vs competition; model consumer price elasticity by segment and channel
Redesign pricing architecture with clear price-pack ladders; eliminate value-destructive promotions with <80% efficiency
Implement pricing governance: price realisation dashboard, promotion approval threshold, and monthly price-mix bridge

diagnostic_signals.signal: Net price realisation declining despite list-price increases due to growing trade promotion as % of gross sales
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Promotion efficiency and architecture review will improve net price

diagnostic_signals.signal: Promotion return on investment <80 paise per rupee of investment for >30% of trade promotions
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Eliminating/redesigning low-ROI promotions will improve contribution

diagnostic_signals.signal: Price gaps vs key competitors narrowing in premium segments where brand premiumisation is the strategy
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Pricing architecture adjustment will protect premiumisation strategy

required_data_fields: SKU-level gross sales, trade promotion, and net sales waterfall by channel
Promotion ROI data by promotion type and channel
Competitor RSP and price-pack architecture by segment
Consumer price sensitivity research by segment
Channel margin structure (distributor, retailer) by format

condition_precedents: Distributor profitability analysis completed by channel / region
Trade promotion ROI measured and low-ROI programmes identified
Sales force restructured per new RTM design
industry_sources: FICCI — Trade and Distribution Policy Report 2024 (ficci.in) — GTM cost benchmarks for FMCG India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit current trade terms by channel and distributor class: primary discount, secondary schemes, credit period, and total trade spend %
Benchmark trade terms against category norms and key account negotiation data; build terms simplification model
Redesign trade terms with fewer schemes, performance-linked payouts, and shorter credit periods; pilot with mid-tier distributors first
Roll out new terms structure; track trade spend as % of net sales, DSO, and distributor profitability quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Total trade spend (primary + secondary schemes) >12% of gross sales vs FMCG benchmark of <10%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Trade terms rationalisation will improve net realisation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Number of active trade schemes >20 per quarter creating complexity and leakage
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scheme simplification will reduce administrative cost and misclassification risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Distributor credit period >45 days with receivables overdue >30 days representing >10% of outstanding
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Credit term tightening will improve working capital and bad-debt risk

required_data_fields: Trade spend breakdown by channel and scheme type (primary/secondary)
Distributor margin structure and credit terms by class of trade
Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) by channel and key account
Scheme ROI data (sales lift vs scheme cost)
Competitor trade terms benchmark (qualitative/channel insights)

condition_precedents: Volume throughput exceeds automation break-even (> 500 picks/hr typical)
WMS system upgrade completed to support automation integration
Capex approval obtained (typically > 18-month payback)
industry_sources: CRISIL — India Logistics and Warehousing Report 2024 (crisil.com) — warehouse automation adoption and cost savings benchmarks
McKinsey & Company — Automation in Warehouse Operations (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations) — 15-30% labour cost reduction through automation
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map current warehouse operations: inbound, storage, pick-pack, and outbound; measure labour hours, error rate, and cost per case picked
Build automation ROI model (ASRS, conveyor, sorting, WMS) based on volume, SKU count, and labour cost; compare against greenfield vs retrofit
Select technology vendor; execute WMS upgrade and automation equipment deployment in waves starting with highest-volume fulfilment centre
Track throughput per hour, error rate (pick accuracy), and cost per case picked post-automation; rebaseline annually

diagnostic_signals.signal: Labour cost as % of total warehouse cost >50% with cost per case picked >₹8 vs automated benchmark <₹4
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation ROI is positive on 3-year DCF basis

diagnostic_signals.signal: Pick error rate >0.5% generating downstream returns and customer service cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will reduce error-related cost and improve service level

diagnostic_signals.signal: Warehouse capacity utilisation >90% seasonally constraining fulfilment throughput
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation and space optimisation will extend throughput without additional sq ft

required_data_fields: Warehouse labour cost and FTE count by facility and operation type
Throughput data: orders per day, cases per picker-hour, and peak vs average volume
Pick error and returns rate by SKU category
Warehouse footprint and lease terms by facility
Automation technology vendor options and capex estimates

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Logistics Mode Shift (Air → Road / Rail / Ocean)
condition_precedents: Lead time analysis confirms alternative mode feasible for customer SLA
Inventory buffer stock adjusted to accommodate longer lead times
Carrier contracts updated for new modes at agreed rates
industry_sources: DPIIT — Logistics Ease Across Different States (LEADS) Report 2023 (dpiit.gov.in) — modal mix and freight cost benchmarks India
CRISIL — India Logistics Report 2024 (crisil.com) — road vs. rail freight cost differential
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Baseline freight spend by mode (road, rail, air, sea) and lane; calculate cost per tonne-km and transit time by mode
Model modal shift feasibility by lane: rail vs road for >800 km lanes, coastal shipping for port-to-port, air-to-surface for non-urgent loads
Negotiate long-term contracts with rail (CONCOR/private) and coastal shipping operators; optimise carrier mix per lane
Track freight cost per tonne-km, modal split, transit time, and OTIF by lane monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Air or premium express freight >10% of total freight spend for non-time-sensitive loads
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Mode shift to surface/rail will reduce freight cost without impacting service

diagnostic_signals.signal: Road freight on lanes >800 km where rail alternative exists and service level is acceptable
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rail or coastal modal shift will reduce cost per tonne-km by 30-50%

diagnostic_signals.signal: Freight cost as % of net sales trending upward for ≥3 consecutive quarters
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural review of logistics network and mode mix is required

required_data_fields: Freight spend by mode, lane, and carrier (12 months)
Volume (tonnes) and transit time by lane and mode
Rail/coastal shipping network connectivity to key origin-destination pairs
Carrier rate cards and contract terms by mode
Inventory holding cost and service-level trade-off data

condition_precedents: Consumer acceptance testing completed for private label alternatives
Contract manufacturing partner qualified and audited
Shelf placement and channel strategy agreed with commercial team
industry_sources: RAI — India Retail Report 2024 (rai.net.in/) — private label penetration and margin benchmarks for Indian organized retail
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map category white space: identify segments where private label penetration <10% and competitor PL or NBD is gaining share
Qualify manufacturers for PL production: supplier audit, GMP compliance, formulation capability, and exclusivity terms
Launch PL range in pilot stores (>20 stores) with consumer testing panel; track consumer acceptance vs brand alternative
Scale to full chain; track PL penetration, category margin uplift, and branded manufacturer response

diagnostic_signals.signal: Category gross margin <25% with private label equivalent achievable at >35% margin on same shelf
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Private label will structurally improve category margin mix

diagnostic_signals.signal: Private label penetration in category <15% vs modern trade benchmark of >25% in comparable markets
diagnostic_signals.confirms: PL expansion headroom is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Consumer willingness to trade down confirmed by market research for this category
diagnostic_signals.confirms: PL launch risk is manageable

required_data_fields: Category margin data by brand and pack size
Private label penetration by category and store format
Consumer research on PL acceptance by category
Manufacturer shortlist with capacity and compliance data
Competitor PL pricing and ranging data

condition_precedents: Demand planning tool (S&OP / IBP) implemented or upgraded
Forecast accuracy KPI established and tracked at SKU/DC level
Safety stock policy revised to statistical model (not rule-of-thumb)
industry_sources: Gartner — Supply Chain Top 25 2024 (gartner.com/en/supply-chain/insights/supply-chain-top-25-research) — demand planning accuracy and inventory reduction benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Measure current forecast accuracy (MAPE, bias) by SKU and channel vs benchmark; identify root causes of forecast error
Implement statistical forecasting model (ARIMA/ML) for top-80% revenue SKUs; integrate sell-in and sell-out data from key retailers
Redesign S&OP process: weekly demand review with commercial inputs, 13-week rolling forecast, and agreed consensus number
Track MAPE improvement, excess inventory reduction, and OTIF; reward commercial team on forecast accuracy alongside volume

diagnostic_signals.signal: Weighted MAPE >30% for top-50 SKUs indicating significant forecast error and resulting inventory/service trade-off
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Improved forecasting will reduce safety stock requirement and excess inventory write-off

diagnostic_signals.signal: Excess and obsolete inventory write-off >1% of net sales annually due to overforecasting
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Forecast improvement will directly reduce write-off cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: OTIF (On Time In Full) <95% due to demand-supply mismatch driven by poor forecast accuracy
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Better planning will improve service level without incremental inventory

required_data_fields: SKU-level forecast vs actual data (12 months) with MAPE and bias calculation
Excess and obsolete inventory write-off by SKU and cause
Sell-out data from key retail accounts or Nielsen/IRI
S&OP meeting cadence and consensus forecast process description
Safety stock policy and inventory days-on-hand by SKU

condition_precedents: SKU velocity and margin analysis completed by channel
De-listing plan approved by commercial, supply chain and finance
Customer communication plan executed for discontinued SKUs
industry_sources: Nielsen — Assortment Optimisation in Retail 2024 (nielsen.com/insights/) — tail elimination and shelf productivity benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build planogram analysis for top-20% stores by revenue: map space allocation vs space-to-sales ratio by SKU and category
Identify ranging inefficiencies: under-ranged high-demand SKUs and over-spaced slow-movers; model impact of rebalancing
Negotiate category captain agreements with key suppliers for planogram execution support; implement ranging decisions by store format
Track sales per sq ft uplift, in-stock rate, and category profitability improvement post-range change

diagnostic_signals.signal: Sales per sq ft below category benchmark for ≥30% of floor space allocation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Space rebalancing will improve category return per sq ft

diagnostic_signals.signal: Out-of-stock rate >5% for top-50 SKUs while slow-movers occupy allocated space
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Assortment rebalancing will improve in-stock rate and consumer basket value

diagnostic_signals.signal: Category profitability below retailer target for ≥2 consecutive quarters despite adequate sales
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Assortment mix shift to higher-margin SKUs will improve category profit

required_data_fields: SKU-level sales per sq ft by store format and location
Planogram space allocation by SKU and category
In-stock (OSA) monitoring data by SKU and store
Category margin by SKU and pack size
Consumer basket composition and cross-purchase data

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
